revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Govern de Hokkaidō]][[ja:北海道庁]]
[[File:Hokkaido Prefectural Office03s5s4272.jpg|thumb|300px|Former Main Office Building (Red Brick) (Photo taken in October 2009)]] [[File:Hokkaido Government Office Building in Meiji era.JPG|thumb|200px|Hokkaido Government Office in the Meiji Era]]
'''Hokkaidō-chō''' (北海道庁) was the administrative agency overseeing the region of {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}, established in {{ill|1886|en|1886|qid=Q7822|lt=1886|1=1886}} (Meiji 19) and dissolved in {{ill|1947|en|1947|qid=Q5263|lt=1947|1=1947}} (Shōwa 22). It was a local administrative agency directly under the {{ill|Interior ministry|en|Interior ministry|qid=Q6589202|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|1=Interior ministry}}.
== Overview == Under the Director of Hokkaidō-chō, there were the Director’s Secretariat, the General Affairs Department, the Education Department, the Economic Department, the Public Works Department, the Colonization Department, and the {{ill|Hokkaido Government Police Department|ja|北海道庁警察部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Government Police Department|1=Hokkaido Government Police Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11402735}}, which shared administrative duties. Due to historical circumstances, Hokkaido's legal position differed from other prefectures. The {{ill|Hokkaido Regional Expenses Law|ja|北海道地方費法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Regional Expenses Law|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Meiji 34 Law No. 3) treated the "Hokkaido Regional Expenses" as a quasi-public entity akin to other prefectures. Furthermore, the {{ill|Hokkaido Assembly Law|ja|北海道会法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Assembly Law|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Meiji 34 Law No. 2) established the Hokkaido Assembly (the equivalent of today's {{ill|Hokkaido Legislative Assembly|en|Hokkaido Legislative Assembly|qid=Q11403065|lt=Hokkaido Prefectural Assembly|1=Hokkaido Legislative Assembly}}) and the Hokkaido Council. Many provisions of the {{ill|Prefecture System (Japan)|ja|府県制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefecture System (Japan)|1=fukensei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486098}} were a11lied mutatis mutandis to these laws.
== History == * 1869 (Meiji 2) ''' August 15 (July 8 in the lunar calendar): The {{ill|Hokkaidō Development Commission|en|Hokkaidō Development Commission|qid=Q524428|lt=Kaitakushi|1=Hokkaidō Development Commission}} was established. ''' September 20 (August 15 in the lunar calendar): The region, formerly known as {{ill|Wajinchi|ja|和人地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wajinchi|1=Wajinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11417141}} and {{ill|Ezo|en|Ezo|qid=Q1040184|lt=Ezochi|1=Ezo}}, was renamed {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido (Ritsuryō)|1=Hokkaido}} with 11 provinces and 86 districts established within its boundaries. * 1870 (Meiji 3), March 14 (February 13 in the lunar calendar): Renamed "Hokkaido Kaitakushi" following the establishment of the {{ill|Karafuto Kaitakushi|ja|樺太開拓使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karafuto Kaitakushi|1=Karafuto Development Commission|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11543658}}. * 1871 (Meiji 4), September 21 (August 7 in the lunar calendar): Reintegrated with Karafuto Kaitakushi, reverting to Kaitakushi. * 1882 (Meiji 15), February 8: The Kaitakushi was abolished, and {{ill|Three Prefectures Era|ja|三県一局時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Prefectures Era|1=Three prefectures period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10896699}} (Hakodate Prefecture, Sa11oro Prefecture, Nemuro Prefecture) was established ({{ill|Haishichiokiken|ja|廃使置県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haishichiokiken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三県一局時代|1=Three prefectures period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10896699}}). * 1886 (Meiji 19), January 26: The three prefectures were abolished, and Hokkaidō-chō was established, with the main office in Sa11oro and branches in Hakodate and Nemuro ({{ill|Haikenchichō|ja|廃県置庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haikenchichō|qq=}}). * 1893 (Meiji 26): Colonel {{ill|Gunji Naritada|ja|郡司成忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji Naritada|1=Shigetada Gunji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6275615}}, {{ill|Nobu Shirase|en|Nobu Shirase|qid=Q715832|lt=Shirase Nobu|1=Nobu Shirase}}, and others launched the Kurile reclamation project, including wintering teams on {{ill|Shumshu|en|Shumshu|qid=Q838500|lt=Shumshu Island|1=Shumshu}} and nearby islands. * 1897 (Meiji 30), November 2: {{ill|Subprefectures of Japan|en|Subprefectures of Japan|qid=Q850450|lt=Subprefectures of Hokkaido|1=Subprefectures of Japan}} were established, and district offices were abolished. * 1903 (Meiji 36), December: {{ill|Shana Subprefecture|ja|紗那支庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana Subprefecture|1=Shanashi Subprefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605484}} was merged into {{ill|Nemuro Subprefecture|ja|根室支庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nemuro Subprefecture|1=Nemuro Subprefecture (1897—2010)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q839319}}. * 1947 (Shōwa 22) ''' April 21: In the first unified local elections, {{ill|Toshibumi Tanaka|en|Toshibumi Tanaka|qid=Q11575576|lt=Toshifumi Tanaka|1=Toshibumi Tanaka}} was elected as the first publically chosen director. ''' May 3: With the enactment of the {{ill|Local Autonomy Act|en|Local Autonomy Act|qid=Q2480675|lt=Local Autonomy Law|1=Local Autonomy Act}}, Hokkaidō-chō was abolished, and the {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido Prefecture|1=Hokkaido}} was established as a regular local government. The Director of Hokkaidō-chō was renamed Governor of Hokkaido.
== List of Directors == {{See|List of Governors of Hokkaido}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Former Hokkaidō Government Office|en|Former Hokkaidō Government Office|qid=Q861554|lt=Former Hokkaido Government Office|1=Former Hokkaidō Government Office}} * {{ill|Hokkaido Railway|ja|北海道官設鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Railway|1=Hokkaidō Government Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11402686}} - Operated by the Railway Department of Hokkaido-chō and incorporated into the {{ill|Imperial Government Railways|ja|鉄道作業局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Government Railways|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鉄道省|1=Ministry of Railways (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q249654}} in 1905. * {{ill|Hokkaido Development Agency|ja|北海道開発庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Development Agency|1=Hokkaido Development Agency|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11403713}} * {{ill|Hokkaido Development Bureau|ja|北海道開発局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Development Bureau|1=Hokkaido Regional Development Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11403712}} * {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} - The current administrative authority * {{ill|Hokkaido (Ritsuryō)|ja|北海道 (令制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido (Ritsuryō)|1=Hokkaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11287110}}
{{s-start}} {{s-bef|before={{ill|Three Prefectures Era|ja|三県一局時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Prefectures Era|1=Three prefectures period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10896699}}|表記=前}} {{s-ttl|title=Administrative Transition|years={{ill|1886|en|1886|qid=Q7822|lt=1886|1=1886}} - {{ill|1947|en|1947|qid=Q5263|lt=1947|1=1947}}}} {{s-aft|after={{Flagcountry|Hokkaido}} {{SSR}}}} {{end}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|北海道庁|version=103938294|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[image:Michitoshi Iwamura.jpg|120px|thumb|right|First Director Michitoshi Iwamura]]
[[ja:北海道庁]]
[[ja:北海道庁 (1886-1947)]]
